schemes.name: Pradhan Mantri Kisan Samman Nidhi (PM-KISAN)
schemes.purpose: Supplement financial needs of land holding farmers via DBT of Rs. 6000 per year.
schemes.eligibility: Indian citizens who are landholding farmer families owning cultivable agricultural land. Excludes institutional landholders, high-income earners, and taxpayers.
schemes.application_process: Apply online via pmkisan.gov.in under 'Farmers Corner -> New Farmer Registration' or offline through Common Service Centres (CSCs).
documents_required: Bank Account Details (Aadhaar seeded with IFSC)
schemes.mandatory_requirements: Aadhaar authentication (eKYC) is mandatory. Land must be recorded in the applicant's name in state records, and the bank account must be seeded with Aadhaar for Direct Benefit Transfer (DBT).

schemes.name: Pradhan Mantri Kisan MaanDhan Yojana (PM-KMY)
schemes.purpose: Provide Rs. 3,000 monthly pension to enrolled small and marginal farmers (SMFs) post 60 years of age.
schemes.eligibility: Small and Marginal Farmers (SMFs) owning cultivable land up to 2 hectares. Must be between 18 to 40 years of age.
schemes.application_process: Online self-registration via the Maandhan portal or offline assisted registration via Common Service Centres (CSCs).
documents_required: Savings Bank Account / PM-KISAN Account details
schemes.coverage_risks: Old age financial insecurity and loss of livelihood.
schemes.mandatory_requirements: Age strictly between 18 to 40 years at the time of entry, and regular monthly contributions to the pension fund.

schemes.name: Pradhan Mantri Fasal Bima Yojana (PMFBY)
schemes.purpose: Provide simple, affordable, and comprehensive crop insurance against non-preventable natural risks.
schemes.eligibility: All farmers growing notified crops in notified areas, including sharecroppers and tenant farmers.
schemes.application_process: Apply online via the National Crop Insurance Portal (NCIP), PMFBY mobile app, or offline through bank branches, CSCs, or authorized insurance agents during the sowing season.
documents_required: Land Possession Certificate (LPC) / Records of Right (RoR)
schemes.coverage_risks: Yield losses due to natural fire, lightning, storms, hail, cyclone, drought, flood, pests, and diseases. Also covers prevented sowing, post-harvest losses (up to 14 days), and localized calamities.
schemes.mandatory_requirements: Must have an insurable interest in the crop, timely premium payment (1.5% for Rabi, 2% for Kharif, 5% for commercial), and application within designated crop cycle windows.

schemes.name: Modified Interest Subvention Scheme (MISS)
schemes.purpose: Provide concessional short-term agri-loans up to Rs. 3.00 lakh at 7% (effectively 4% with prompt repayment).
schemes.eligibility: Farmers availing short-term crop loans through Kisan Credit Card (KCC) for crop husbandry and allied activities.
schemes.application_process: Apply for or renew a Kisan Credit Card (KCC) at any participating commercial bank, cooperative bank, or Regional Rural Bank (RRB).
schemes.coverage_risks: Not Applicable (Provides relief from high-interest debt and financial distress).
schemes.mandatory_requirements: Must possess a Kisan Credit Card (KCC) and ensure prompt repayment of the loan within one year to receive the additional 3% subvention.
where_to_apply_links: Apply offline at local Bank Branches

schemes.name: Agriculture Infrastructure Fund (AIF)
schemes.purpose: Medium-long term debt financing (Rs. 1 lakh crore) for post-harvest management infrastructure with 3% interest subvention.
schemes.eligibility: Farmers, FPOs, PACS, Agri-entrepreneurs, Startups, State Agencies, and Local Body sponsored PPP projects.
schemes.application_process: Register on the AIF portal, generate credentials via OTP, fill out the application form, and upload a Detailed Project Report (DPR). The application is verified by the Ministry PMU and automatically forwarded to the chosen lending institution.
schemes.coverage_risks: Credit guarantee coverage provided under CGTMSE for eligible loans up to Rs. 2 Crore.
schemes.mandatory_requirements: Project must strictly be for post-harvest management infrastructure or community farming assets.

schemes.name: Formation & Promotion of new 10,000 FPOs
schemes.purpose: Form and promote 10,000 FPOs with financial assistance, matching equity grants, and credit guarantees.
schemes.eligibility: Groups of farmers willing to aggregate and form a Farmer Producer Organization.
schemes.application_process: Farmers must approach designated Implementing Agencies (IAs like NABARD, SFAC, NCDC) or local Cluster Based Business Organizations (CBBOs) for group registration and handholding.
schemes.mandatory_requirements: Minimum 300 farmers required to form an FPO in plain areas, or 100 farmers in Hilly/North-Eastern regions.

schemes.name: National Beekeeping and Honey Mission (NBHM)
schemes.purpose: Promote scientific beekeeping and achieve the 'Sweet Revolution'.
schemes.eligibility: Individuals, beekeepers, farmers, FPOs, and honey societies engaging in beekeeping and related activities.
schemes.application_process: Register online via the Madhukranti portal. Project proposals for funding must be routed through State Departments of Agriculture/Horticulture or nodal agencies.
schemes.mandatory_requirements: Must be actively engaged in scientific beekeeping, honey production, or honey processing.

schemes.name: Market Intervention Scheme and Price support Scheme (MIS-PSS)
schemes.purpose: Procure pulses, oilseeds, copra, and perishables to protect growers from distress sales during bumper crops.
schemes.eligibility: Growers of specific agricultural and horticultural commodities when market prices fall below economic levels.
schemes.application_process: Farmers register at designated state procurement centers (like NAFED, FCI, or state agencies) during the officially announced procurement window.
documents_required: Crop registration slip from state portal
schemes.coverage_risks: Protects farmers from market price crashes, distress sales, and unremunerative pricing during peak harvest arrivals.
schemes.mandatory_requirements: Agricultural produce must meet Fair Average Quality (FAQ) standards set by the government.
where_to_apply_links: State-specific e-Procurement Portals / Local Mandi Boards

schemes.name: Namo Drone Didi
schemes.purpose: Provide drones to 15,000 Women SHGs for rental agricultural services (80% subsidy).
schemes.eligibility: Women-led Self-Help Groups (SHGs) registered under DAY-NRLM. Members must be between 18-40 years old (varies slightly by state) with basic educational qualifications.
schemes.application_process: Selection is cluster-based. SHGs apply via the Central Agri Mechanization DBT Portal (agrimachinery.nic.in) or through the State Rural Livelihood Mission (SRLM) offices.
schemes.mandatory_requirements: At least one SHG member must successfully complete a mandatory 15-day training (5 days DGCA drone pilot training + 10 days agricultural application training).

schemes.name: Rastriya Krishi Vikas Yojana - DPR
schemes.purpose: Create pre & post-harvest infrastructure with state autonomy.
schemes.eligibility: State Governments, State agencies, and Agri-startups seeking funding for agriculture and allied sector projects.
schemes.application_process: Startups apply through designated RKVY-RAFTAAR incubators. State departments submit Detailed Project Reports (DPRs) to the State Level Sanctioning Committee (SLSC).
schemes.mandatory_requirements: Project must align with state agricultural priorities and receive official SLSC approval.

schemes.name: Soil Health Card (SHC)
schemes.purpose: Provide information to farmers on soil nutrient status and fertilizer recommendations.
schemes.eligibility: All farmers across India possessing cultivable land.
schemes.application_process: Submit soil samples to local agriculture department officials, Krishi Vigyan Kendras (KVKs), or approved soil testing labs.
schemes.coverage_risks: Not Applicable (Mitigates soil degradation and excessive chemical fertilizer use).
schemes.mandatory_requirements: Must own or cultivate the land from which the soil sample is taken.

schemes.name: Rainfed Area Development (RAD)
schemes.purpose: Promote Integrated Farming System (IFS) in cluster mode to mitigate extreme weather impacts.
schemes.eligibility: Farmers located in rainfed areas adopting integrated farming systems.
schemes.application_process: Apply through the local District Agriculture Office or State Agriculture Department based on local cluster planning.
schemes.coverage_risks: Mitigates the impacts of extreme weather events like droughts and floods by diversifying agricultural income.
schemes.mandatory_requirements: Must adopt an Integrated Farming System (combining multi-cropping with livestock, fishery, apiculture, etc.).
where_to_apply_links: State Agriculture Department Portals / Local District Agriculture Office

schemes.name: Per Drop More Crop (PDMC)
schemes.purpose: Increase farm-level water use efficiency via micro-irrigation technologies.
schemes.eligibility: All farmers, with special provisions and higher subsidies for Small/Marginal farmers, SC/ST, and women.
schemes.application_process: Apply through the State's horticulture/agriculture department portal or physically at the District Agriculture Office by submitting land details and vendor quotations.
documents_required: Quotation from approved micro-irrigation system vendor
schemes.coverage_risks: Mitigates water scarcity, drought impacts, and inefficient nutrient usage.
schemes.mandatory_requirements: The micro-irrigation system (drip/sprinkler) must be purchased from and installed by a government-registered/approved vendor.

schemes.name: Micro Irrigation Fund (MIF)
schemes.purpose: Facilitate states in mobilizing resources for micro-irrigation via NABARD. (Merged with PDMC).
schemes.eligibility: State Governments (beneficiaries are ultimately farmers receiving state subsidies).
schemes.application_process: States apply to NABARD for funds. Farmers apply for the resulting subsidies via their state-specific agriculture departments.
schemes.mandatory_requirements: State governments must commit to mobilizing the resources to expand micro-irrigation coverage.

schemes.name: Paramparagat Krishi Vikas Yojana (PKVY)
schemes.purpose: Increase soil fertility and healthy food production via organic practices in cluster mode.
schemes.eligibility: Groups of at least 20 farmers forming a cluster of 20 hectares to transition to organic farming.
schemes.application_process: Farmers must form a cluster and apply through the local Gram Panchayat or District Agriculture Office for registration under the Participatory Guarantee System (PGS).
schemes.mandatory_requirements: Farmers must strictly avoid agro-chemicals and follow approved organic practices to achieve certification.
where_to_apply_links: Local Gram Panchayat / District Agriculture Office

schemes.name: Sub-Mission on Agriculture Mechanization (SMAM)
schemes.purpose: Accelerate agricultural mechanization, especially for small/marginal farmers.
schemes.eligibility: Individual farmers, SHGs, FPOs, and cooperatives looking to purchase agricultural machinery or establish Custom Hiring Centres (CHCs).
schemes.application_process: Apply online via the Direct Benefit Transfer (DBT) portal for Agricultural Mechanization (agrimachinery.nic.in) by uploading required documents and vendor quotes.
schemes.mandatory_requirements: Purchase must strictly be from approved manufacturers/dealers listed on the central portal.

schemes.name: Crop Residue Management
schemes.purpose: Prevent crop residue burning by promoting in-situ management machinery. (Merged with SMAM).
schemes.eligibility: Farmers, cooperative societies, FPOs, and panchayats, primarily in Punjab, Haryana, UP, and Delhi.
schemes.application_process: Apply via state-specific agricultural mechanization portals or the central agrimachinery.nic.in portal to get subsidies on specific CRM equipment.
schemes.coverage_risks: Mitigates severe air pollution and prevents the loss of soil micro-organisms caused by crop burning.
schemes.mandatory_requirements: Machinery must be used strictly for in-situ management of crop residue.

schemes.name: Agro-forestry
schemes.purpose: Promote plantation on farmlands by providing Quality Planting Materials (QPM).
schemes.eligibility: Farmers willing to plant trees on their farmland alongside crops.
schemes.application_process: Farmers can apply through the State Forest Department or District Agriculture Office to receive subsidized saplings.
schemes.mandatory_requirements: Willingness to integrate tree plantation with existing crop farming operations.
where_to_apply_links: State Forest Department / District Agriculture Offices

schemes.name: National Food Security Mission (NFSM)
schemes.purpose: Increase production of cereals and pulses through area expansion and productivity enhancement.
schemes.eligibility: Farmers in identified districts growing rice, wheat, pulses, coarse cereals, and nutri-cereals (millets).
schemes.application_process: Benefits (like subsidized seeds and inputs) are routed through the State Agriculture Department. Farmers apply at the Block/District Agriculture Office.
schemes.mandatory_requirements: Must be cultivating the specific notified crops in the targeted districts.
where_to_apply_links: State Agriculture Department Portals / Local Block Agriculture Office

schemes.name: Sub-Mission on Seed and Planting Material (SMSP)
schemes.purpose: Support seed production chain and infrastructure. (Merged with NFSM).
schemes.eligibility: Seed producing organizations, state farms, and farmers involved in certified seed production.
schemes.application_process: Participation is coordinated by State Seed Corporations and the State Agriculture Department. Monitored via the SATHI portal.
documents_required: Registration with State Seed Certification Agency
schemes.mandatory_requirements: Strict adherence to scientific seed production quality, testing, and isolation guidelines.

schemes.name: National Mission on Edible Oils (NMEO)-Oil Palm
schemes.purpose: Promote oil palm cultivation for edible oil self-reliance.
schemes.eligibility: Farmers in designated target states (especially North-East and A&N Islands) willing to cultivate oil palm.
schemes.application_process: Apply through the District Horticulture/Agriculture Officer or state-designated agencies handling the Oil Palm mission.
schemes.mandatory_requirements: Land must be geographically and climatically suitable for oil palm cultivation.

schemes.name: Mission for Integrated Development of Horticulture (MIDH)
schemes.purpose: Holistic growth of the horticulture sector.
schemes.eligibility: Farmers, FPOs, and entrepreneurs engaged in cultivating fruits, vegetables, spices, flowers, and aromatic plants.
schemes.application_process: Apply through the State Horticulture Mission (SHM) portal or directly at the District Horticulture Office with a project proposal.
schemes.mandatory_requirements: Must be actively engaged in cultivating horticultural crops or setting up post-harvest horticulture infrastructure.

schemes.name: National Bamboo Mission (NBM)
schemes.purpose: Develop complete value chain of the bamboo sector. (Merged with MIDH).
schemes.eligibility: Farmers, artisans, and entrepreneurs involved in bamboo plantation or bamboo product manufacturing.
schemes.application_process: Apply through the State Bamboo Mission (SBM) or State Bamboo Development Agency (SBDA) at the district level.
schemes.mandatory_requirements: Activities and projects must fall strictly within the bamboo value chain.

schemes.name: Integrated Scheme for Agriculture Marketing (ISAM)
schemes.purpose: Govern agricultural produce marketing via infrastructure and e-NAM integration.
schemes.eligibility: Farmers, traders, FPOs, and state APMCs.
schemes.application_process: Farmers and traders can register on the e-NAM portal (enam.gov.in) or via the e-NAM mobile app to participate in online trading.
schemes.mandatory_requirements: Must belong to or trade within an APMC mandi integrated with the e-NAM network.

schemes.name: Mission Organic Value Chain Development for North Eastern Region (MOVCDNER)
schemes.purpose: Develop certified organic production clusters in the NE region.
schemes.eligibility: Farmers in the 8 North Eastern states willing to form FPOs/FPCs for organic farming.
schemes.application_process: Implemented through State Lead Agencies. Farmers apply by joining cluster-level FPOs mobilized by state-appointed agencies.
schemes.mandatory_requirements: Must reside in one of the 8 North Eastern states and commit strictly to certified organic farming practices.
where_to_apply_links: State Lead Agencies / Agriculture Departments in NE Region

schemes.name: Sub-Mission on Agriculture Extension (SMAE)
schemes.purpose: Disseminate technology to farmers via ATMA and digital initiatives.
schemes.eligibility: All farmers seeking technical advisory, training, and exposure visits.
schemes.application_process: Contact the Agricultural Technology Management Agency (ATMA) at the district level or register via digital portals like Kisan Call Centre and VISTAAR.
documents_required: Basic farmer profile details (to register for SMS advisories)
schemes.mandatory_requirements: None. Open to all farmers for knowledge dissemination.

schemes.name: Digital Agriculture
schemes.purpose: Develop a digital public infrastructure (AgriStack) for inclusive, farmer-centric solutions.
schemes.eligibility: All farmers with official land records (for inclusion in the unified Farmers Database).
schemes.application_process: No direct application required. Farmers are onboarded during state-run digital crop surveys and registry processes (eKYC).
documents_required: Land records (to be linked for generating the unique Farmers ID)
schemes.mandatory_requirements: Successful eKYC linkage with official state land records.
